result:
If I could change the world in one way, I’d improve humanity’s ability to cooperate across differences.

So many problems—poverty, war, climate change, public health, misinformation—are made worse not just by lack of knowledge, but by mistrust, polarization, short-term incentives, and inability to coordinate. If people and institutions became better at seeking truth, building trust, compromising fairly, and acting for the long term, a lot of other improvements would become much easier.

content.text:

So many problems—poverty, war, climate change, public health, misinformation—are made worse not just by lack of knowledge, but by mistrust, polarization, short-term incentives, and inability to coordinate. If people and institutions became better at seeking truth, building trust, compromising fairly, and acting for the long term, a lot of other improvements would become much easier.

prompt: If you could change the world in one way, what would it be?
